description: Fix data loss bugs in network-capture.ts where getRequests(clear=true) and getWsFrames(clear=true) destroy internal lookup maps needed by ongoing CDP event handlers, silently dropping subsequent HTTP responses and WebSocket frames until new connections are created.

userStories.title: Preserve lookup maps when clearing network capture buffers
userStories.description: As a developer using browser_get_network_requests or browser_get_websocket_frames with clear=true, I want the clear operation to only remove completed entries from the output buffers so that in-flight HTTP requests and existing WebSocket connections continue to be captured correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: getRequests(tabId, clear=true) clears the `requests` output array but does NOT clear `pendingRequests` — in-flight HTTP requests that have not yet received a response continue to be tracked and appear in subsequent getRequests calls

getRequests(tabId, clear=true) removes completed entries from `requestIdToRequest` (entries that are in the returned `requests` array) but preserves entries for in-flight requests still in `pendingRequests`

getWsFrames(tabId, clear=true) clears the `wsFrames` output array but does NOT clear `wsFramesByRequestId` or `wsCreatedAt` — existing WebSocket connections continue to have their frames captured and appear in subsequent getWsFrames calls
Unit tests verify that after clear, new events from in-flight HTTP requests are still captured
Unit tests verify that after clear, new WebSocket frames from existing connections are still captured
userStories.notes:
File: platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.ts

Bug 1 — getRequests (line 527-542):
When clear=true, line 538 calls `state.pendingRequests.clear()`. This destroys the Set of requestIds for HTTP requests that sent `Network.requestWillBeSent` but haven't received `Network.responseReceived` yet. The `responseReceived` handler at line ~236 checks `if (!pending) return;` — so any in-flight request's response data is silently dropped after clear.

Line 539 also clears `requestIdToRequest`, which is needed by `loadingFinished`/`loadingFailed` handlers to finalize requests. In-flight requests will lose their response data.

Fix for getRequests:
- Remove `state.pendingRequests.clear()` from the clear path entirely — these are in-flight requests that haven't completed
- For `requestIdToRequest`, only delete entries whose requestIds are NOT in `pendingRequests` (i.e., completed requests). Simplest approach: rebuild requestIdToRequest from only the pending entries after clearing.

Bug 2 — getWsFrames (line 577-588):
When clear=true, lines 584-585 clear `wsFramesByRequestId` and `wsCreatedAt`. These maps store the requestId→URL mapping populated by `Network.webSocketCreated` events. The frame handlers (`webSocketFrameSent`, `webSocketFrameReceived`) at lines ~343-362 look up `state.wsFramesByRequestId.get(requestId)` — after clear, ALL subsequent frames from existing WebSocket connections are silently dropped because the lookup returns undefined.

Fix for getWsFrames:
- Do NOT clear `wsFramesByRequestId` or `wsCreatedAt` when clear=true. Only clear the `wsFrames` output array. The lookup maps are lightweight (one entry per WebSocket connection) and are needed for ongoing frame capture.
- `wsFramesByRequestId` entries are already cleaned up by `webSocketClosed` handler, so they don't leak.

These maps ARE properly cleaned up in stopCapture() (line ~382-404) which tears down the entire capture session. The clear operation should only reset the output buffers, not the internal bookkeeping state.

Existing tests are in platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.test.ts — add test cases that verify capture continues working after clear.
